rationale: Apache ActiveMQ's Java process (parent, A0) spawned cmd.exe to use certutil for downloading a randomly-named executable (qSwUwejx.exe) from an internal attacker-controlled HTTP server (10.0.2.13:8080), consistent with CVE-2023-46604 or similar ActiveMQ RCE exploitation (A0, EV-003, EV-004). The downloaded payload was immediately executed (EV-005, EV-006) and within ~2 seconds established a TCP connection to 10.0.2.13:4444 — a classic reverse-shell port — confirming C2 callback (EV-007). The payload then spawned a cmd.exe shell (EV-008, EV-009), demonstrating interactive post-exploitation access, and subsequently used certutil again to download AnyDesk from the internet (EV-011, EV-012), a well-known persistence/remote-access technique used by threat actors to maintain access. The full kill-chain — RCE → payload download → execution → C2 callback → interactive shell → remote-access tool installation — is confirmed across multiple evidence items with no benign explanation.
